subject: Revert "Using AAF release Frankfurt dockers"
commit_message:
Revert "Using AAF release Frankfurt dockers"

This reverts commit 306fa85f8e97e1e7f95f8b429088f570f2926d39.
Since this patch, we're seeing a lot of errors on healtchecks and e2e
tests.
Reverting it in order to make master work again.

Issue-ID: AAF-1127
Signed-off-by: Sylvain Desbureaux <sylvain.desbureaux@orange.com>
Change-Id: Ie40559ef5a3da60457b0af306b802e7a81d968be

entries.message:
Patch Set 1:

Gitlab controller: Deployment put on queue, number 1 on the queue.

Patch Set 1:

I'm proposing a revert on the previous patch as it seems to be the one preventing SDC distribution to work well

Patch Set 1: Verified+1

Build Successful 

https://jenkins.onap.org/job/oom-master-verify-helm/5006/ : SUCCESS

Logs: https://logs.onap.org/production/vex-yul-ecomp-jenkins-1/oom-master-verify-helm/5006

Patch Set 1:

Gitlab Azure 1: Chained-CI build started, max time: 14400 seconds

Patch Set 1: Code-Review+2

Hmm OK my fault I didn't catch that. I thought that it's rather a glitch as OOF CMSO was also failing and it happen in dozens of reviews. If this revert helps I leave you my +2 so that you can merge it as soon as gating passes

Patch Set 1:

mmmh interesting, I suspect credentials mismatch somewhere (getting 403s is a good sign of that - either in AAF data or robot creds or components or both), the main changes in AAF 2.1.22 is about pre provisionned data (the initial creds). The process that was put in place in former releases (detailled here : https://wiki.onap.org/display/DW/AAF+Initialization+Data) is to get a golden image from the lab to be sure that the setup is as close to what teams have been testing against. 

One way to verify it, would be to get audit logs from AAF from the gating run (aaf service pod, audit logs), to try and catch unauthorized creds and their origin)

Patch Set 1:

> Patch Set 1:
> 
> mmmh interesting, I suspect credentials mismatch somewhere (getting 403s is a good sign of that - either in AAF data or robot creds or components or both), the main changes in AAF 2.1.22 is about pre provisionned data (the initial creds). The process that was put in place in former releases (detailled here : https://wiki.onap.org/display/DW/AAF+Initialization+Data) is to get a golden image from the lab to be sure that the setup is as close to what teams have been testing against. 
> 
> One way to verify it, would be to get audit logs from AAF from the gating run (aaf service pod, audit logs), to try and catch unauthorized creds and their origin)

Unfortunately, AAF is following the (awkward) rule to not print on STDOUT so logs are lost forever...

will try to catch them in our daily master (when it finishes)

Patch Set 1:

Gitlab Azure 1: Chained CI build failure on some jobs.
Jobs with issue:
  * name: apps_test:onap_oom_gating_azure_1, no url

Patch Set 1:

Gitlab Azure 1: 
Full results can be seen here: https://gating-results.onap.eu/results//108222-1/index.html

Patch Set 1:

Build Successful 

https://jenkins.onap.org/job/oom-master-merge-helm/1638/ : SUCCESS

Logs: https://logs.onap.org/production/vex-yul-ecomp-jenkins-1/oom-master-merge-helm/1638

Patch Set 1:

> Patch Set 1: Code-Review+2
> 
> Healthdist is up again. 
> @morgan, any reason why pnf registrate fails?

argh you merged, I have no access to the logs anymore

but we have also an issue on frankfurt today https://gating-results.onap.eu/results/onap_daily_pod4_frankfurt-567030023-05-26-2020_02-05/smoke-usecases-robot/pnf-registrate/pnf-registrate/report.html#totals?critical
2/3 PASS with 1 FAIL on modeling part

the ipaddress-v4-oam field retrieved from AA&I is empty 13.13.13.13 was expected
AA&I answered properly but it seems that the IP provided through the VES is not used anymore
the VES request looks correct, it includes this IP
but it seems to be dropped when we try to get it when calling the AAI API to check that the registration is done.

I checked it was working on the frankfurt daily of the 20th - we got some turbulences during the ascension break - no results, yesterday it was not executed and today we got this error.

Note on the 20th the only change on AAI (service-model) was already there

@Krzysztof K, you confirm?

we got one PASS today on a patch (108039-1 - merged)
http://testresults.opnfv.org/onap/api/v1/results?case_name=pnf-registrate&last=10&criteria=PASS
the previous PASS are from the 22nd

Patch Set 1:

> Patch Set 1:
> 
> mmmh interesting, I suspect credentials mismatch somewhere (getting 403s is a good sign of that - either in AAF data or robot creds or components or both), the main changes in AAF 2.1.22 is about pre provisionned data (the initial creds). The process that was put in place in former releases (detailled here : https://wiki.onap.org/display/DW/AAF+Initialization+Data) is to get a golden image from the lab to be sure that the setup is as close to what teams have been testing against. 
> 
> One way to verify it, would be to get audit logs from AAF from the gating run (aaf service pod, audit logs), to try and catch unauthorized creds and their origin)

I agree it can be a misalignment between AAF, robot tests and/or other components,it is hard to guess as we are not very clean on the dependencies..
Healthcheck tests are "relatively trustable" and should help identifying such issues.

When AAF sneezes, the rest of ONAP catches a cold :)
